title: M5/M8: the steelman filter is a critical, adversarial-review gate ('only findings that survive the steelman go in the result', review.py:320-321), but the result contract has no field to record steelmanned-away or near-proposed findings or the reasoning for rejection. A future reviewer cannot tell whether a non-finding was properly steelmanned or simply overlooked, and there is no re-surfacing mechanism for discarded candidates (unlike UNADOPTED items, which the follow-up loop re-surfaces).
context: reviews/2026-06-11-04: missed because The protocol emphasises steelmanning in instructions but designed the result contract to capture only what survives, treating the steelman as a thought process rather than an artifact. Verification confirmed the asymmetry: review_gaps carry a why_missed trail for surviving findings, but rejected candidates leave none; distinct from the adjacent gap lume-0014 (multi-viewpoint swarm) which proposes a structurally different remedy.; proposed: Tracked as an optional spike in the review-protocol-auditability-and-scale workstream: evaluate a lightweight 'steelmanned-away / near-finding' note (capture the rejection rationale so future reviews can re-check whether it still holds) against the protocol's deliberate single-pass minimalism. Low priority — a discarded finding can be independently re-found by a future thorough review.
